summary.intent: Fix the dcg-guard hook silently passing through all commands when `dcg` is not on the gateway process PATH, particularly under systemd where $HOME/.local/bin is stripped from PATH.
summary.outcome: All three changes were implemented (PATH augmentation in handler, loud stderr warning, startup log, updated HOOK.md), 4 unit tests added and passing, fmt/clippy clean, bd issue created/closed, and PR committed and pushed.

repo: dcg-guard hook runs in the gateway process environment, not inside any sandbox — hooks always inherit the gateway's PATH via ShellHookHandler (shell_hook.rs:101-117)
The `which` crate is available as a workspace dependency in moltis; can be used for binary resolution without spawning a subprocess
Existing hook seeder tests pattern exists in the tests mod at server.rs:4778+; new tests follow the same tempdir-based pattern
bd CLI is used for issue tracking: `bd create` then `bd update --status=in_progress` then `bd close`
The project uses `just lint` wrapping clippy with nightly-2025-11-30 toolchain and `--features local-llm-metal` flag
CLAUDE.md rule: commit only when explicitly asked — changes were left staged in working tree after implementation

code.finding: DCG_GUARD_HANDLER_SH constant containing the hook shell script — was fail-open silent no-op; now prepends PATH and warns loudly
seed_dcg_guard_hook() function — augmented to call log_dcg_guard_status() helper using same PATH as handler
DCG_GUARD_HOOK_MD constant — seeded HOOK.md that incorrectly referenced `cargo install dcg` (which doesn't work)
bin_exists() pattern used as reference for binary resolution idiom in this codebase
ShellHookHandler spawns sh -c inheriting gateway process env — root cause of narrow PATH under systemd

workflow: End-to-end shell testing with `env -i` to simulate minimal systemd PATH is effective for validating PATH augmentation fixes without needing a real systemd environment
Running `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all` (without --check) first, then re-running with --check to verify, is more reliable than fixing fmt issues manually
Background task notifications from earlier runs can arrive late and be safely ignored when the same command already completed in the foreground

friction: A stale background task notification arrived after the work was already complete, requiring recognition that it was a duplicate of a foreground result
The `cargo install dcg` instruction in the seeded HOOK.md was misleading — dcg is a Python tool installable only via uv/pipx, not cargo; this was a pre-existing doc bug compounding the issue
open_items: Manual verification checklist items deferred to operator: delete ~/.moltis/hooks/dcg-guard/ and restart Moltis to confirm startup log line appears; test actual rm -rf blocking with dcg installed vs uninstalled
CI does not run the end-to-end shell test (PATH simulation with env -i) — only unit tests run in CI; E2E test is documented as 'run locally' only
No docs page for dcg-guard/hooks exists yet in docs/src/ — the plan noted this but no page was created since grep confirmed none existed
